title: Trump's tariffs, Fed minutes, Rumble's Bitcoin buy - what's moving markets
Gold edges lower to near $4,100 on renewed US–Iran tensions
summary: Gold price (XAU/USD) loses ground to around $4,100 during the early Asian session on Wednesday. The precious metal faces new selling pressure after the US vows a response against Iran following reports of attacks on three oil tankers in and around the Strait of Hormuz.

title: United States Dollar Index holds above 101.00 following US strikes on Iran
summary: The US Dollar Index (DXY), which measures the value of the US Dollar (USD) against six major currencies, is holding gains for the second consecutive day and trading around 101.10 during the Asian session on Wednesday.

title: India Gold price today: Gold rises, according to FXStreet data
summary: Gold prices rose in India on Wednesday, according to data compiled by FXStreet.

title: Silver climbs above $60.00 despite US–Iran tensions, Fed minutes in focus
summary: Silver price (XAG/USD) attracts some buyers to near $60.35, snapping the two-day losing streak during the early European session on Wednesday. The white metal edges higher amid a softer US Dollar (USD) ahead of the release of the June FOMC meeting Minutes.

title: Gold retains positive bias above $4,100; upside seem limited ahead of FOMC Minutes
summary: Gold (XAU/USD) sticks to its modest intraday gains above the $4,100 mark through the Asian session on Wednesday and, for now, seems to have snapped a two-day losing streak, reaching the weekly trough touched the previous day.

title: Morgan Stanley cautious on BioMar as input inflation clouds earnings
